channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: Pope Leo XIV's flight home from Spain was grounded so the king came to his aid
item.description: Leo's Iberia charter, due to take him back to Rome after a weeklong visit to Spain, was grounded by a technical problem Friday, prompting Spain's king to offer his private jet instead.
item.content:encoded:
Leo's Iberia charter, due to take him back to Rome after a weeklong visit to Spain, was grounded by a technical problem Friday, prompting Spain's king to offer his private jet instead.

(Image credit: Vatican Media via AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump says U.S. military strike killed leader of Tren de Aragua gang
item.description: President Trump said Friday that a U.S. strike has killed Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, whom he called "the infamous leader" of the Tren de Aragua gang in Venezuela.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump said Friday that a U.S. strike has killed Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, whom he called "the infamous leader" of the Tren de Aragua gang in Venezuela.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Gene Shalit, longtime 'Today' show movie critic, dies at 100
item.description: Known for his puffy hair, oversized handlebar mustache and a love for puns, Gene Shalit joined Today in 1970 and became arts editor in 1973. He was a middle-of-the-road critic, known for his wit and intelligence.
item.content:encoded:
Known for his puffy hair, oversized handlebar mustache and a love for puns, Gene Shalit joined Today in 1970 and became arts editor in 1973. He was a middle-of-the-road critic, known for his wit and intelligence.

(Image credit: Richard Drew)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump's name is still on the Kennedy Center — officials say it will be down by noon
item.description: President Donald Trump's name remained on the facade of the Kennedy Center early Saturday despite a court-ordered Friday deadline to remove references to Trump from the building.
item.content:encoded:
President Donald Trump's name remained on the facade of the Kennedy Center early Saturday despite a court-ordered Friday deadline to remove references to Trump from the building.

(Image credit: Cliff Owen)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Justice Dept. approves Paramount's acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery
item.description: The decision paves the way for a proposed $111 billion merger uniting two rival studio giants — Paramount, owner of CBS, and the much larger Warner, which includes HBO and CNN.
item.content:encoded:
The decision paves the way for a proposed $111 billion merger uniting two rival studio giants — Paramount, owner of CBS, and the much larger Warner, which includes HBO and CNN.

(Image credit: Nick Ut)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: A Trump push to cut 'statistical noise' could mean less data from the Census Bureau
item.description: New public data for redistricting and other uses may be reduced as Trump officials limit the ways the Census Bureau can protect people's privacy when it releases statistics.
item.content:encoded:
New public data for redistricting and other uses may be reduced as Trump officials limit the ways the Census Bureau can protect people's privacy when it releases statistics.

(Image credit: Anton Petrus)
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang

item.title: What would it take to stop women from bleeding to death after childbirth?
item.description: A newly published series of reports calls attention to a dire situation facing millions of women after childbirth — and the solutions that can prevent death from postpartum hemorrhage.
item.content:encoded:
A newly published series of reports calls attention to a dire situation facing millions of women after childbirth — and the solutions that can prevent death from postpartum hemorrhage.

(Image credit: Lynsey Addario)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: To read more this summer, stop waiting for the perfect moment
item.description: So you want to ignite a reading habit this summer. How do you get back into the groove? We talk to reading enthusiasts for their best tricks — like allowing yourself to read wherever, whenever.
item.content:encoded: So you want to ignite a reading habit this summer. How do you get back into the groove? We talk to reading enthusiasts for their best tricks — like allowing yourself to read wherever, whenever.
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: China arrests a U.S. scholar with a history of Myanmar activism, suspected of spying
item.description: China's government said Min Zin, who heads a think tank focused on Myanmar, was detained on suspicion of engaging in "espionage and endangering Chinese national security."
item.content:encoded:
China's government said Min Zin, who heads a think tank focused on Myanmar, was detained on suspicion of engaging in "espionage and endangering Chinese national security."

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Wai Moe

item.title: 4 hands on the keys: The continuing piano adventures of the fearless Labèque sisters
item.description: The French pianists celebrate more than a half century of recording together with a triple-disc set containing many brand new tracks.
item.content:encoded:
The French pianists celebrate more than a half century of recording together with a triple-disc set containing many brand new tracks.

(Image credit: Umberto Nicoletti)
item.dc:creator: Tom Huizenga
